content.title: Engineering Discovery: Improving architecture from 70 to 90+ will increase platform
content.abstract: This paper presents findings from a controlled experiment testing the hypothesis: "Improving architecture from 70 to 90+ will increase platform reliability by ≥15%.". Analysis of 2 evidence points yielded outcome: requires_follow_up (confidence: 60%).

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
sections.body: Platform telemetry revealed a researchable pattern in the engineering domain. The hypothesis was generated from source: benchmarks.

sections.heading: 2. Hypothesis
sections.body:
Improving architecture from 70 to 90+ will increase platform reliability by ≥15%.

Measurable: architecture score ≥90 after intervention
Falsifiable: If architecture score does not increase by ≥10 points after 2 weeks of changes, the hypothesis is refuted.

sections.heading: 3. Methodology
sections.body: Experiment type: before-after analysis. Evidence collected from 2 platform sources. Execution mode: automated via Autonomous Execution Engine.

sections.heading: 4. Results
sections.body: Outcome: requires_follow_up with 60% confidence.

sections.heading: 5. Conclusions
sections.body: The hypothesis requires further investigation. Follow-up experiment recommended.

sections.heading: 6. Platform Impact
sections.body: Current engineering baseline: {"architecture":70,"code_quality":75,"maintainability":70,"reliability":75,"security":80,"scalability":65,"performance":70,"overall":73}. Open patterns: 14.
